title: News shirtwoot Eva 222 T Shirt Derby
display_title: News shirtwoot Eva 222 T Shirt Derby
summary:
[]: [News] shirt.woot Eva 2.22 T-Shirt Derby             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Mar 17, 2011 10:51 am Those who have a knack for Photoshop and graphics, shirt.woot has a contest to design a new shirt for Eva 2.22: http://shirt.woot.com/evangelion222 Starts on March 18 (that's tomorrow). Read contest guidelines to be sure. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva M

chunks.content:
Article: News shirtwoot Eva 222 T Shirt Derby
[]: [News] shirt.woot Eva 2.22 T-Shirt Derby             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Mar 17, 2011 10:51 am Those who have a knack for Photoshop and graphics, shirt.woot has a contest to design a new shirt for Eva 2.22: http://shirt.woot.com/evangelion222 Starts on March 18 (that's tomorrow). Read contest guidelines to be sure. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 17, 2011 11:21 am My mind is blown.

[]: This contest is as pointless as Rei's baby-smooth butt.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Mar 17, 2011 3:24 pm shirt.woot wrote: Do not use any images, logos, or other assets associated with Evangelion. We're looking for "Robots Save the World" t-shirts, not Evangelion t-shirts.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 17, 2011 3:36 pm I'm actually stumped on what's the point of a Eva t-shirt design contest if you're not supposed to reference the show/images somehow... (Like, how would the shirt even look then? Just a generic robot outline instead of an outline of Eva-01 or something?)

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Mar 17, 2011 3:55 pm As if they could afford the license for Eva imagery, duh.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 17, 2011 4:08 pm I just meant that lots of places tend to have a silhouette of a character or characters if they're under copyright. For instance, unofficial guidebooks of anime/whatever else tend to do this. (I know there was one unofficial/fan-written Sailormoon book that had an outline of the 5 main sailor senshi for instance.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 17, 2011 4:13 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: As if they could afford the license for Eva imagery, duh.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Mar 17, 2011 9:46 pm Checking the discussions/comments section down below revealed that several artists put

 could afford the license for Eva imagery, duh.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Mar 17, 2011 9:46 pm Checking the discussions/comments section down below revealed that several artists put out their own interpretations without using any official licenses: http://shirt.woot.com/Forums/ViewPost.aspx?PostID=4387066 Basically, mostly old-school metallic giant robot designs.
